response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist engineer with a long track record across full-stack, embedded, and high-throughput systems, and he clearly meets the raw years-of-experience bar. However, the role is tightly scoped around experimentation platform engineering — A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging, telemetry pipelines, rollout risk controls, and statistical bias detection — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His high-scale systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/second) is a positive signal, but it is framed as low-latency data integration rather than growth or measurement infrastructure. The gap between his background and the core requirements of this Principal-level role is too large to bridge with a generalist interview.
strengths: Polyglot engineer with production experience in JavaScript/TypeScript, C++, C#, Java, and DLang — covers most of the required language list
Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second in a microservices architecture
Staff-level contributions, community teaching (workshops, talks), and open-source project ownership show the leadership floor expected at Principal level
concerns: Zero visible experimentation platform experience — no A/B testing, feature flagging, experiment assignment, bias detection, or rollout risk management anywhere in the resume
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure background; the role requires ownership of telemetry foundations and cross-surface funnel measurement as core deliverables
No evidence of cross-team, stakeholder-alignment, or growth-systems work; the role requires driving multi-workstream initiatives across Product, Data Science, and Research
